WARNING: This data is ONLY for CSE and CS branch.
prerequisite: Basic knowledge on Computer System and system memory, Computer Organization and Logic Design (COLD)
course_objectives: Provide comprehensive understanding of operating systems, their components, structures, and functionalities.
Cover process management, memory management, file systems, and concurrency.
Emphasize practical aspects: system calls, Linux commands, shell scripting, and scheduling algorithms.
course_outcomes: CO1: Understand the need, evolution and design issues of various categories of operating systems.
CO2: Apply different CPU scheduling algorithms and deadlock handling methods.
CO3: Analyze the principles of concurrency control and process synchronization problem.
CO4: Analyze various memory management techniques for efficient memory allocation.
CO5: Apply the concept of various I/O management, Disk scheduling and file system.

units.title: Introduction of Operating System
topics: Operating system Components and its services
Classification: Batch system, Time sharing, Real Time System, Multiprocessor, Multiuser, Multiprocess, Multithreaded
OS Structure: Layered structure, Reentrant Kernels, Monolithic and Microkernel Systems
System Calls
Elementary Linux commands and Shell Scripting

units.title: Process Scheduling and Resource Management
topics: Process States, State Transition Diagram
Schedulers, Process Control Block (PCB)
Threads and their management
CPU Scheduling: Concepts, Performance Criteria, Scheduling Algorithms
Deadlock: System model, characterization, Prevention, Avoidance and detection, Recovery

units.title: Concurrent Processes
topics: Principle of Concurrency
Critical Section Problem, Mutual Exclusion
Dekker's solution, Peterson's solution
Semaphores, Monitors, Test and Set operation
Classical Problems: Producer/Consumer, Reader Writer, Dining Philosopher, Sleeping Barber
Inter Process Communication models (IPC)

units.title: Memory Management
topics: Basic bare machine, Resident monitor
Multiprogramming with fixed partitions and variable partitions
Protection schemes, Paging, Segmentation, Paged segmentation
Virtual memory concepts, Demand paging
Performance of demand paging
Page replacement algorithms
Thrashing

units.title: I/O Management and Disk Scheduling
topics: File Systems and I/O Management of Windows and Linux
Disk storage structure and disk scheduling algorithms
RAID
Case Study: Introduction to Android and Mac Operating System
The Evolution of Mobile Operating Systems: iOS vs Android

textbooks: Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne - Operating Systems Concepts, Wiley
Harvey M Dietel - An Introduction to Operating System, Pearson Education
William Stallings - Operating Systems: Internals and Design Principles, 6th Edition, Pearson Education
reference_books: Sibsankar Halder and Alex A Aravind - Operating Systems, Pearson Education
D M Dhamdhere - Operating Systems: A Concept based Approach, 2nd Edition, TMH
Andrew S. Tanenbaum and Herbert Bros - Modern Operating Systems (4th Edition), Pearson
